title: Ful Medames

overview.description: A traditional Egyptian breakfast of gently seasoned fava beans topped with fried eggs and served with warm flatbread. Nutritious, economical and surprisingly filling.

method: Heat 1 tablespoon of the olive oil in a saucepan over a medium heat.
Cook the garlic for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the cumin and coriander and cook for 30 seconds.
Add a splash of water if needed and simmer gently for 8–10 minutes.
Lightly mash some of the beans while leaving plenty whole.
Stir in the lemon juice and season with salt and black pepper.
Meanwhile, heat the remaining olive oil in a frying pan.
Fry the eggs to your preferred level of doneness.
Divide the beans between four bowls.
Top each portion with a fried egg.

learning.did-you-know: Ful Medames has been eaten in Egypt for thousands of years and remains one of the country's most popular breakfasts.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to season beans simply with spices and lemon, and how to create texture by partially mashing them.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An inexpensive breakfast that introduces Egyptian cooking while making excellent use of pantry ingredients.
chapman-tips: Don't mash all of the beans—the mixture should have plenty of texture.
Fresh lemon juice is essential for balancing the earthy flavour of the beans.
Warm the bread just before serving.
If you can't find tinned fava beans, dried beans cooked until soft work beautifully.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze the bean mixture for up to three months. Fry fresh eggs when serving.
notes.leftovers: The bean mixture also makes an excellent lunch served in warm pita.
